title: Add parsing to image manifest if result is not manifest list
body:
This modification aims to even the behavior between the cli and the REST
API.

When retrieving a manifest of an single image via
```
podman manifest inspect custom.registry.com/container:v0.1
```

we obtain the following warning: `The manifest type
application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json is not a manifest
list but a single image.` and the content of the manifest. 

ex: 
```
$ podman manifest inspect docker.io/williamyeh/dummy:latest
WARN[0001] Warning! The manifest type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json is not a manifest list but a single image.
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
    "config": {
        "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json",
        "size": 1629,
        "digest": "sha256:f5891d1355691307d37711001b329fd1a8b193f41fd9011ce6b914cf6d2818b0"
    },
    "layers": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip",
            "size": 723172,
            "digest": "sha256:f70adabe43c0cccffbae8785406d490e26855b8748fc982d14bc2b20c778b929"
        }
    ]
}
```

When using the REST API with the socket, we receive an empty manifest
response like the following:
```
$ curl --unix-socket ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/v2/libpod/manifests/docker.io/williamyeh/dummy:latest/json
{"schemaVersion":2,"mediaType":"application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json","manifests":null}
```

The goal of this patch is to have the following response from the REST API:
```
$ curl --unix-socket ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/v2/libpod/manifests/docker.io/williamyeh/dummy:latest/json
{"schemaVersion":2,"mediaType":"application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json","config":{"mediaType":"application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json","size":1629,"digest":"sha256:f5891d1355691307d37711001b329fd1a8b193f41fd9011ce6b914cf6d2818b0"},"layers":[{"mediaType":"application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip","size":723172,"digest":"sha256:f70adabe43c0cccffbae8785406d490e26855b8748fc982d14bc2b20c778b929"}]}
```

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body: @restanrm I think this is only implemented for `api`  we would also need this for `cli` and test cases around this. You can refer to similar test cases on `test/e2e`.

> @restanrm I think this is only implemented for `api` we would also need this for `cli` and test cases around this. You can refer to similar test cases on `test/e2e`.

The CLI already returns the content of the manifest if the container is a single image. I don't mind writing test cases, I'll come back to you when it's finished !

I've added tests for the REST part in the second commit of this PR. I hope that the image `quay.io/libpod/busybox:latest` is stable enough to be used in tests. I saw that it's already used like this, but didn't found confirmation of this. 

For the CLI the test is here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a2acd04447a66027dc02a210d2961fcde6868985/test/e2e/manifest_test.go#L68-L71
This seems like it might be better-done in the frontend, given that this doesn't seem to preserve the "no manifest was found" error?

> This seems like it might be better-done in the frontend, given that this doesn't seem to preserve the "no manifest was found" error?

The warning is still printed in the `podman system service` terminal. 

You mean that the warning shouldn't exist or that I should create another specific endpoint to retrieve single image manifest ?
Changes LGTM, but our CI requires that PRs are signed.  Please `git commit --amend -s`, fetch, rebase and push.
Thanks @TomSweeneyRedHat is that ok now ?
/assign @giuseppe

> This seems like it might be better-done in the frontend, given that this doesn't seem to preserve the "no manifest was found" error?

@mheon I am entirely not sure but this also serves the REST API right, so isn't this check is fine at backend since similar request could also directly made to the API instead of `podman-remote` assuming you meant `podman-remote` client by frontend. Correct me if i am wrong.

Yes, but that doesn't mean we shouldn't do the check in the frontend. We're still going to lose the error, and return something that is decidedly not a manifest. The frontend should be responsible for checking if there was an error retrieving the manifest, and instead inspecting the image.

> Yes, but that doesn't mean we shouldn't do the check in the frontend. We're still going to lose the error, and return something that is decidedly not a manifest. The frontend should be responsible for checking if there was an error retrieving the manifest, and instead inspecting the image.

@mheon oh i see that makes sense.

>  Yes, but that doesn't mean we shouldn't do the check in the frontend. We're still going to lose the error, and return something that is decidedly not a manifest. The frontend should be responsible for checking if there was an error retrieving the manifest, and instead inspecting the image.

@mheon What do you mean by “frontend” in this case ? Do you expect me to do some modifications to the PR ?
Friendly ping @mheon

Sorry, missed this.

Frontend = the Go bindings for the API (pkg/infra/tunnel, the CLI code for the REST API) - so the check will be done on the client side, as opposed to right now on the server side.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@restanrm Are you still working on this?
Honestly no, i'm not. If I understand the point of view, I have nothing to do since it should be done on client side. However I didn't remember having an endpoint to get image manifests.
@flouthoc or @mheon could you finish this PR?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Ping @flouthoc

@mheon @rhatdan Could we close this PR. I will pick @restanrm 's commit and open a new PR will add @restanrm as co-author. Only If we are fine with closing this PR and @restanrm is not interested in continuing this PR, I'll also check with what `moby` API is returning for this.
It's ok with me ! Thanks !
